FX: 1 USD = 9.72 SEK today. US prices/values are SEK-normalised; limit_price_sek should be the SEK-equivalent.

JOURNAL (current thesis state):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Portfolio at +0.3%, severely behind floor pace (-9.3pp on day 35). The weekly rotation (ATRLJ + HM-B exit, VIMIAN + LIFCO entry) has deployed most idle cash; now at 31.9% cash with 8 holdings spanning five sectors. The critical earnings cluster lands in 27–33 days: VOLV/ESSITY (Jul 24), HEXA (Jul 25), LIFCO (Jul 24), VIMIAN (Jul 29), STORY-B (Jul 29). No further cash deployment until ATRLJ's Q2 (Jul 10, already exited) and the first print lands. The book is now fully positioned to adjudicate on thesis quality; execution is clean, conviction is real, margin is thin.

## 2. Holdings

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Consumer Staples | 6.7%
- **Thesis:** 512 MSEK insider cluster (7 buyers, 0 sellers, Industrivärden lead) into a defensive compounder at ~14x P/E, 3.4% yield. Q2 Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 reveals accelerating revenue decline.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Industrials | 3.9%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK near 52w highs endorsing forward earnings on net cash, 4% yield, 9.8% op margin. Q2 Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Insider becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order-intake collapse.
- **Status:** `intact`

**RUSTA.ST** — Consumer Discretionary | 3.8%
- **Thesis:** Aforber Invest bought ~52 MSEK across 13 tranches into -23% drawdown post-positive Q4 and dividend raise. Discount retail counter-cyclical tailwind. Starter pending D/E leverage validation (likely IFRS-16 operating lease).
- **Sell if:** Genuine financial leverage >243% unconfirmed, same-store sales deteriorate, or Aforber becomes net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

**STORY-B.ST** — Consumer Discretionary | 15.0%
- **Thesis:** 45 MSEK insider cluster (founder + CFO) into post-downgrade dip. Jul 29 catalyst; high conviction on timing and insider quality.
- **Sell if:** Q2 reveals margin compression or revenue miss; insider becomes net seller.
- **Status:** `intact` — largest conviction position

**SCST.ST** — Consumer Staples | 6.4%
- **Thesis:** Broad 9-buyer insider cluster (0 sellers, CEO + strategic owner) on a staples name. Starter ahead of Q2 earnings.
- **Sell if:** Q2 shows material margin or revenue deterioration; insiders turn net seller.
- **Status:** `intact` — newest addition, unproven

**HEXA-B.ST** — Industrials | 6.1%
- **Thesis:** Unanimous 4-buyer cluster (CFO included) into 21% below 200MA washout. Jul 25 catalyst; high vol (82%) provides asymmetry. Data quality flag on fundamentals (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%) not yet resolved.
- **Sell if:** Q2 reveals going-concern deterioration; data cleans up showing the valuation was an artifact but multiples are actually stretched.
- **Status:** `intact` — data risk, Jul 25 print resolves

**VIMIAN.ST** — Health Care | 13.6%
- **Thesis:** 128 MSEK insider cluster led by Fidelio anchor-holder doubling up; reclaiming 200MA. Leveraged compounder with double-digit growth (R12 +11.7%) but deeply negative FCF (-243 MEUR) and 2.8bn EUR net debt. Q2 Jul 29 is binary catalyst; thesis lives on insider + momentum, dies on earnings miss or covenant risk revealed.
- **Sell if:** Q2 shows FCF deterioration (worse than -243 MEUR implied), net debt up, or insiders turn net seller.
- **Status:** `intact` — high conviction on insider signal, real balance-sheet risk

**LIFCO-B.ST** — Industrials | 12.6%
- **Thesis:** Clean insider cluster (11.9 MSEK net buy, 3 buyers incl. repeat board member) on quality 18% op-margin compounder. Jul 24 catalyst. Valuation is rich (P/E 38.8) but insider buying + momentum inflection + earnings season justify the starter.
- **Sell if:** Q2 misses or guidance disappoints; momentum rolls back below 200MA; insider selling accelerates.
- **Status:** `intact` — modest upside skew but real signal

## 3. Watchlist

**ASSA-B.ST** — Quality large-cap compounder; CEO + management bought ~30 MSEK (Apr/Jun), but revenue contracting (-1.8% YoY) and P/E 24x offers no safety margin. Entry target ≤310 SEK post-earnings (Jul 28) or on a deeper dip. Jul 24 VOLV/ESSITY earnings will set tone for industrials/defensives.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Herenco bought ~25 MSEK (3 tranches, Jun) into -24% drawdown; small-cap (16M turno) with soft fundamentals (revenue -3.2% YoY). Strong insider signal but no catalyst until Aug 5 earnings; skip for now due to low liquidity and late print. Revisit post-Aug earnings if position works.

**VIMFAC** — Parent of Vimian; holding company structure. Lower leverage, but watch whether Vimian's Q2 implies a parent-level dividend cut or capital restructuring.

## 4. Lessons / open questions

- **Rotation execution clean.** ATRLJ sold at 26.20 (limit 26.00, filled better); HM-B at 166.05 (limit 165.00, filled better). Market offered discounts on both exits — correct to be patient on limits.
- **Cash burn rapid into earnings cluster.** Deployed ~33k SEK this week; now 31.9% cash. Next cycle should evaluate whether to hold that ~40k or deploy further, contingent on how the first July prints land (VOLV/ESSITY, HEXA, LIFCO Jul 24–25). No action until we see data.
- **Consumer Discretionary concentration:** STORY-B (15%) + RUSTA (3.8%) + previous exits of Thule/HM mean sector is now smaller but STORY-B is outsized. Is this conviction or concentration risk? Answer: conviction on STORY-B insider cluster and Jul 29 catalyst justifies the size; RUSTA is starter. If STORY-B thesis breaks, rotate immediately.
- **Earnings cluster sequencing:** VOLV/ESSITY/LIFCO/HEXA all land Jul 24–25. If the first two disappoint, Industrials and Staples risk repricing downward. Monitor correlation — may need to trim or pivot if that sector reprices hard.
- **Open:** SCST.ST and HEXA-B.ST are both 1-day-old entries with real data risk (HEXA fundamentals possibly an artifact, SCST unproven). Both depend entirely on Q2 earnings to validate thesis. Assess confidence vs. position size — neither is oversized, both are defensible on insider signal alone, but the data gaps are material. Jul 25–29 prints will resolve.

## Daily decisions log

- 2026-06-29 sell 15 VOLV-B.ST @ 324.60 SEK — Scout-flagged weak/flat holder, trimming crowded Industrials to fund fresher insider clusters
- 2026-06-29 buy 350 KJELL.ST @ 12.20 SEK — best-in-class insider cluster (CEO, CFO, largest shareholder) into Jul 24 catalyst, funded partly by VOLV exit
- 2026-06-29 buy 15 SCST.ST @ 145.40 SEK — adding to intensifying controlling-owner + 9-insider cluster ahead of ~Jul 28 print
- 2026-06-30 buy 200 VIMIAN.ST @ 29.65 SEK — deploying idle cash into highest-conviction held insider cluster (Fidelio anchor) ahead of binary Jul 29 catalyst, taking position to ~18.5%


ANALYST NOTES on today's candidates:
--- Analyst note on HM-B.ST (Hennes & Mauritz) ---
# H&M (HM-B.ST) — Research Note

**Thesis.** The Persson family has bought ~2.5B SEK of H&M stock over 90 days, with a ~1.1B SEK acceleration June 30 — right after a weak Q2 print. That's a genuine founder-conviction signal into weakness, which is exactly the setup that can ignite. But this is a large-cap, structurally struggling retailer (revenue -5.4% YoY, sales "with no trend reversal in sight"), not a small/mid-cap where insider clusters reliably front-run inflections. The insider signal is real; the underlying business is not turning.

**Positives**
- Massive, sustained, single-buyer insider cluster — the controlling family adding aggressively into a bad print.
- Strong cash generation: FCF 23.2B SEK, 8.7% FCF yield, ROE 36.3%, 4.26% dividend yield.
- 12m momentum still +23.8%; stock held flat despite the earnings miss, suggesting the founder buying is absorbing selling.

**Concerns**
- Business is deteriorating: revenue -5.4% YoY, Q2 missed both top and bottom line, "strategy is not working" (DI), Pareto sell rating.
- Heavy balance sheet: net debt 60B SEK, net debt/equity 177% — limits flexibility.
- Persson family buys are partly control/consolidation (moving toward buyout optionality), not purely an undervaluation call — weakens the signal quality.
- P/E 21.6 is not cheap for a declining retailer; analysts see "few reasons to justify" it.
- This is large-cap; the §3 insider edge is explicitly framed for Small/Mid Cap. The signal here is softer than the raw +13.1% flag implies.

**Insider / news read.** Insider signal is unusually strong in magnitude (one buyer, 2.5B SEK, zero sellers) but the news flow around it is negative (miss, sell rating, no reversal). The family is buying *conviction*, but the fundamentals are actively deteriorating — a classic value trap risk vs a genuine floor.

**Price read.** 166.70, -4% vs 200ma, -14% vs 52w high, low vol (20%). Flat post-miss is constructive, but no momentum break yet — it's coiled, not moving.

**Verdict.** Insider conviction is real but the deteriorating fundamentals and large-cap context make this a watch, not a fearless buy — WATCH — not actionable now.

```json
{
  "ticker": "HM-B.ST",
  "name": "Hennes & Mauritz",
  "verdict": "WATCH — not actionable now",
  "conviction": 2,
  "key_positive": "Controlling Persson family bought ~2.5B SEK over 90d, accelerating ~1.1B into a weak Q2, absorbing selling and signaling a floor.",
  "key_concern": "Business is actively deteriorating (revenue -5.4% YoY, missed Q2, 'no reversal in sight') and the stock isn't cheap at P/E 21.6.",
  "would_buy_at": 160.0,
  "would_sell_at": 195.0,
  "notes_for_trader": "Family buys may be control-consolidation, not pure undervaluation — softer signal than headline. No momentum break yet. Reconsider on a decisive move above 200ma (~174) on volume, or a sales stabilization data point. Large-cap, so insider edge is weaker than small/mid-cap."
}
```

--- Analyst note on STORY-B.ST (Storytel) ---
# Storytel (STORY-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Held name, cost 91.00, now 91.95 — flat. The story here is a strong insider signal (founder Wingefors bought ~45M SEK across 4 dates, plus CFO buying) landing *right after* Pareto's downgrade knocked the stock double-digits on Spotify-competition fears. The insider timing looks like a deliberate "we disagree with the bear case" statement. On solid fundamentals (P/E 12.9, net cash, 11% op margin, 6.6% FCF yield) this is a reasonable hold, but the thesis is now a genuine contest between a credible bear catalyst and a credible insider rebuttal.

**Positives**
- Founder + CFO cluster buying ~46M SEK, 3 buyers, zero sellers over 90d — a strong Swedish insider signal, and it came *into* the selloff.
- Cheap for the quality: P/E 12.9, ROE 27%, net cash balance sheet, 6.6% FCF yield.
- 3m momentum still positive (+16%) and price holds +8% above 200MA despite the June wobble.

**Concerns**
- Pareto's downgrade (Spotify entering audiobook streaming) is a *real* competitive threat, not noise — this is the kind of structural risk that can compress the multiple.
- 1m return -9.3% shows the market took the bear case seriously; insiders may be early/wrong.
- High volatility (52%) and a pending buyout situation (union case mentioned) add event risk.
- Earnings in ~28 days — the next print will test whether Spotify pressure is showing in numbers yet.

**Insider / news read.** The insider signal is about as strong as they come: founder deploying 45M SEK across four consecutive days immediately after an analyst-driven drop. Set against that is a substantive, sourced bear thesis (Spotify competition) that management itself only "responded" to rather than rebutting with data. Signal vs. signal.

**Price read.** At 91.95, right at our 91.00 basis and 13% off the 52w high — the Pareto drop was bought back but not fully recovered. Sitting on the 200MA-supported uptrend, no thesis break in the tape.

**Verdict.** Insider conviction offsets the competitive concern, valuation is undemanding, and price action is intact — but Spotify is a live risk into an earnings print. Hold, don't add; watch for rotation if a cleaner idea emerges. `HELD: thesis intact`

```json
}
```

--- Analyst note on KJELL.ST (Kjell Group) ---
# Kjell Group (KJELL.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** This is an insider-driven turnaround momentum play, not a fundamentals play. A dense cluster of high-quality buys — CEO, CFO, board, and a major shareholder (Cervantes/Spotify-founder-linked) putting in 4.25M SEK — landed right as the stock broke +59% over 3m and sits at its 52w high. Insiders are signalling a bottom in a struggling business ahead of Q1 earnings (~23 days out). We hold at +13% with a fresh catalyst tailwind; the setup is intact and the smart money is committing hard here.

**Positives**
- Exceptional insider cluster: 7.3M SEK across 7 buyers including CEO, CFO, and largest shareholder — the strongest possible Swedish insider signal.
- Powerful momentum: +21% 1m, +59% 3m, +42% vs 200ma, pressed against 52w high (-1.4%).
- We're already onside at +13.1% with only 1 day held; catalyst confirmation, not chasing.
- Earnings ~24 July gives a near-term event that insiders appear to be positioning for.

**Concerns**
- Fundamentals are genuinely bad: revenue -10% YoY, operating margin -16.7%, ROE -42%, EPS -5.78, negative FCF. This is a loss-making, shrinking retailer.
- Net debt 463 MSEK / 48% of equity — turnaround has a leverage clock on it.
- Vol30d 64.6% is very high; a violent reversal is possible if Q1 disappoints.
- Thin liquidity (3.0M turnover) — hard to exit fast at size.

**Insider / news read.** The signal is the whole thesis: 6 distinct buyers, zero sellers, CEO/CFO/board/largest-shareholder all buying into the print. News confirms the cluster is real and high-profile. This is the highest-quality insider setup you can get on Swedish small-cap.

**Price read.** At 13.80, pinned to the 52w high after a 59% run, well extended above the 200ma. Momentum is confirmed, not exhausted, but entry here is buying strength — no margin of safety if the earnings signal disappoints.

**Verdict.** Strong insider + momentum, already in book onside — HELD: thesis intact.

```json
{
  "ticker": "KJELL.ST",

  "key_concern": "Underlying business is loss-making and shrinking (rev -10%, op margin -16.7%, net debt 48% of equity) — pure sentiment/turnaround bet.",

  "would_sell_at": 11.50,
  "notes_for_trader": "Momentum+insider setup, not a fundamentals name. Hold and let it run into ~24 July earnings; can add on dips but sizing capped by 64% vol and thin liquidity. Sell trigger: close back below cost basis 12.20 or a disappointing Q1 print."
}
```

--- Analyst note on VIMIAN.ST (Vimian Group) ---
# Vimian Group (VIMIAN.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Animal-health platform with a white-hot insider cluster: 128.6M SEK net buying over 90 days, dominated by Fidelio Vet Holding (~62M SEK across two tranches) plus a broad management cohort. We hold at 29.50, essentially flat (px 29.20). The insider signal is the loudest in the scan and the position is intact — the question is add vs. hold, not exit.

**Positives**
- Largest insider cluster in the scan; a major holder (Fidelio) is aggressively accumulating tens of millions of SEK.
- Broad-based buying — 6 distinct buyers including the CEO — not one lone signal.
- Revenue growing double-digit (+11.7% YoY, +13.6% 3y CAGR) with strong 69% gross margin.
- Price stabilizing: +4.5% 1m, +8.6% 3m, sitting just above 200MA after a brutal 12m (−25.7%).

**Concerns**
- FCF is deeply negative (−243 MEUR, −17.4% yield) — a serious cash-burn / leverage question with net debt of 2,827 MEUR.
- The P/E of 3.5 alongside 5.0% ROE and negative FCF looks like a data artifact (likely a one-off gain distorting EPS); do not treat as "cheap on earnings."
- Still 32.7% below the 52w high — this is a wounded name, not a breakout.
- Note the June 16–18 pattern shows matched buy/sell pairs by the same managers (Ehn, Tajbakhsh, Santarelli, Tullberg) — looks like reshuffling/tax mechanics, which dilutes the "conviction" of that management leg. The real signal is Fidelio's ~62M SEK net accumulation.

**Insider / news read.** Insider signal is genuinely strong once you strip the wash-like management pairs — Fidelio's net buying is the core edge. No material news in 30d, so no thesis-breaking catalyst against us. Earnings ~July 29 is the next real event.

**Price read.** 29.20 vs 29.50 cost — flat, holding the 200MA, recovering off lows. No weakness deepening; the "rotation risk" the Scout flags isn't triggered yet.

**Verdict.** Insider conviction intact, price stable, but negative FCF/leverage caps how hard I'd press before the July 29 print — HELD: thesis intact.

```json
{

}
```

--- Analyst note on AMAT (Applied Materials) ---
# Applied Materials (AMAT) — Research Note

**Thesis.** AMAT is a semiconductor capex leader riding the AI infrastructure buildout, and the stock has gone parabolic: +255% over 12m, +90% over 3m, +42% in the last month alone. The momentum is real and news-supported (new AI chip systems, AR partnership with EssilorLuxottica). But the fundamentals do **not** justify this move — revenue is growing only ~3% YoY while the multiple has exploded to 60x P/E. This is a momentum/melt-up trade, not a fundamentals trade.

**Positives**
- World-class franchise: 49% gross margin, 30% operating margin, 35.6% ROE, net cash balance sheet.
- Genuine secular tailwind — AI chip manufacturing capex is a real multi-year demand driver.
- Fresh product catalysts (AI chip systems, AR glasses platform) landed in the last two weeks and drove 10–11% surges.
- Momentum is textbook: +93% above 200MA, still 10% below 52w high (room, not exhausted top).

**Concerns**
- Valuation is stretched to breaking: **P/E 60.8 on ~3% revenue growth** and 1.0% FCF yield. The price has massively outrun fundamentals.
- 30-day vol is **89.7%** — this is an extremely high-beta name; a snapback of 20–30% would be normal, not exceptional.
- Options activity spike suggests speculative/gamma-driven froth, which unwinds violently.
- Next earnings ~97 days out — no near-term fundamental catalyst to backstop the multiple; the move is sentiment-driven.

**Insider / news read.** No insider signal — US name. News flow is genuinely positive and catalyst-rich (AI systems launch, AR partnership), which explains and partly justifies the recent surge. This is momentum with a narrative, not an empty spike.

**Price read.** At $650.91 the stock is +42% in a month and 10% off its high — this is late-stage momentum, not an entry after a pullback. Chasing here means buying near the top of a vertical move with 90% realized vol.

**Verdict.** Real franchise and real tailwind, but the price has detached from fundamentals and vol is extreme — a starter only on a pullback, not a chase at the highs. `WATCH — not actionable now`

```json
}
```

--- Analyst note on MU (Micron) ---
# Micron (MU) — Research Note

**Thesis.** Micron is the pure-play beneficiary of the AI-memory supercycle. R12 revenue up +167% YoY, net margin 55.9%, operating margin 65.7% — these are extraordinary numbers reflecting a genuine DRAM/HBM shortage with pricing power. At P/E 23 on trailing earnings, the stock is *not* obviously expensive despite the +737% 12m move — the multiple is reasonable because earnings exploded. The debate is whether this cycle peak is now (memory is famously cyclical) or has room to run.

**Positives.**
- Fundamentals are genuinely spectacular: 72.6% gross margin, 65.7% operating margin, $36B FCF, net cash balance sheet.
- Five-year price lock-ins and a GM long-term supply deal signal durable demand, not a fleeting spike.
- P/E of 23 is undemanding for a company growing revenue +167% — the earnings, not the multiple, drove the move.
- Momentum intact: still +134.5% above 200MA, but has cooled to -0.3% over 1m (consolidating, not blowing off).

**Concerns.**
- Memory is deeply cyclical. +737% in 12m means enormous embedded expectation; the next down-leg in DRAM pricing would be violent given vol30d of 124.6%.
- Active antitrust/price-fixing litigation (DRAM cartel, "700% inflation" claim) — a real tail risk to the pricing thesis if it forces price relief.
- CEO admitting "customer price pressure" hints the peak-pricing narrative may be maturing.
- Next earnings ~189 days out — no near-term fundamental catalyst; this rides on macro/sentiment until January.

**Insider / news read.** No insider signal — US name. News flow is net-positive (earnings beat, GM deal, third-most-profitable-US-company framing) but the fresh cartel lawsuits and today's sector-wide dip inject real two-sided risk.

**Price read.** At $1032, down -14.9% from the 52w high, consolidating flat over the past month after a parabolic run. This is a pullback-in-uptrend setup, not an entry at extension — but the vol is punishing.

**Verdict.** Real secular trade with best-in-class fundamentals, but late-cycle timing and litigation risk mean I'd size as a starter, not a full swing. — `BUY — starter`

--- Analyst note on PANW (Palo Alto Networks) ---
# Palo Alto Networks (PANW) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** PANW is a top-tier cybersecurity platform riding the AI-security spend wave, with an earnings beat and raised guidance on June 3. Fundamentals are solid (net cash, 72% gross margin, 19% revenue growth, $3.1B FCF). But the stock has already tripled in 3 months (+119.6%) and sits exactly at its 52w high, +75% above its 200MA. The business is great; the price has run very far, very fast. Upside from here requires paying 296x earnings for continued momentum.

**Positives**
- Raised full-year guidance on AI-related demand (June 3) — a fresh fundamental catalyst.
- Net cash balance sheet, 72% gross margin, $3.1B FCF — durable quality.
- Secular AI + security tailwind is real; new Deutsche Telekom AI-security platform expands TAM.

**Concerns**
- P/E ~296 and FCF yield 1.3% — priced for perfection; almost no valuation cushion.
- +119.6% in 3 months and exactly at 52w high — this is late-stage momentum, not a fresh breakout with room.
- Stock *fell* despite beating on June 3 (Invezz) — a warning that good news is already priced in.
- No earnings catalyst for ~189 days; the near-term move is pure sentiment/flow.

**Insider / news read.** No insider signal — US name. News is net positive (guidance raise, AI platform launch) but the market's negative reaction to a beat is the tell: expectations are extreme.

**Price read.** Textbook extended. +17% in a month, +75% over the 200MA, right at the 52w high with 57% vol. Buying here is chasing — the exact behavior the constitution warns against (§6, don't chase). A pullback toward the 200MA would be a far better entry.

**Verdict.** Great company, exhausted chart at a nosebleed valuation — WATCH, not a chase. WATCH — not actionable now.

--- Analyst note on CAT (Caterpillar) ---
# Caterpillar (CAT) — Research Note

**Thesis** — CAT has ripped +155% in 12 months on an industrial/infrastructure capex + AI-datacenter-power narrative, and now trades at a 49x P/E — a valuation that is extreme for a cyclical machinery maker growing revenue ~12%. The momentum is real but the price has run far ahead of the fundamentals, and a credible short (Burry) just called it. This is a chase, not an edge.

**Positives**
- Powerful, sustained momentum: +14.6% 1m, +39.9% 3m, near 52w high with strong turnover (liquid, tradeable).
- Genuine ROE 50.5% and $8.8B FCF — the underlying business is high-quality and profitable.
- Dividend raised 8% in June — management signaling confidence in cash generation.

**Concerns**
- P/E 49x on a cyclical industrial is nosebleed territory; FCF yield of only 1.9% offers no valuation cushion.
- Net debt/equity 209% — leveraged balance sheet into a rate/cycle-sensitive business.
- Michael Burry disclosed a short and the stock dropped 4% today — sentiment is turning at exactly the wrong price.
- 3y revenue CAGR just +4.4% — nothing about the fundamentals justifies a 155% 12m re-rating; this is multiple expansion on an AI-power story that could unwind fast.

**Insider / news read** — No insider signal (US name). News is mixed-to-negative: dividend hike (positive but minor) offset by Burry's short disclosure and a 4% drop today. The narrative that drove the rally is being challenged publicly.

**Price read** — At $991, 6.9% off the 52w high and 42.8% above the 200MA — deeply extended. Buying a parabolic cyclical at 49x after Burry's short is chasing a crowded trade with no margin of safety.

**Verdict** — Momentum is real but valuation is stretched, the catalyst is turning negative, and I have no honest edge here. PASS — no edge.

response_text:
**Read.** 38% cash, badly below floor pace (-10.1pp) — capital isn't working hard enough. MU is the only actionable new buy (starter). Scout flags ESSITY/SCST as dead weight; both are flat, low-conviction staples. Rotate them into a MU starter and deploy idle cash.

**Decisions.**
- Sell ESSITY-B (8,229) — Scout's weakest holding, flat momentum, staples don't fit the aggressive mandate; frees capital.
- Sell SCST (10,178) — 5d old, flat, thinnest insider signal of the staples; rotated in too early.
- Buy MU starter (~14% / ~21,000 SEK) — best-in-class AI-memory fundamentals (P/E 23 on +167% rev growth, 66% op margin, $36B FCF), consolidating not chasing; funded by the two staples exits. Sizing as starter given 125% vol / cyclical peak risk.

Holding STORY-B, KJELL, VIMIAN, LIFCO, HEXA, RUSTA into their July prints — theses intact. This lifts cash-to-work and adds a genuine US secular name with no home bias. HM-B/AMAT/PANW/CAT all chases at highs — pass. Semi/tech sector stays well under the 40% cap with MU alone.

MU at $1032 × 9.72 = 10,031 SEK/share; ~2 shares ≈ 20,062 SEK (~13.3%).
